sections.title: Reading the signpost at the centre of Canifis
Attempting to pickpocket Dr Fenkenstrain before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: What do you think you're doing???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I could find body parts?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: The soil of Morytania is unique in its ability to preserve the bodies of the dead, which is one reason why I have chosen to carry out my experiments here.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I recommend digging up some graves in the local area. To the south-east you will find the Haunted Woods; I believe there are many graves there.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: There is also a mausoleum on an island west of this castle. I expect the bodies that are buried there to be extremely well preserved, as they were wealthy in life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do, again?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I need you to get me enough dead body parts for me to stitch together a complete body, which I plan to bring to life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...okay...if you insist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you want to make a creature?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I came to the land of Morytania many years ago, to find a safe sanctuary for my experiments. This abandoned castle suited my purposes exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were you experimenting in?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Oh, perfectly innocent experiments - for the good of mankind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why did you need to come to Morytania?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Enough questions, now. Get back to your work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will this creature put me out of a job?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: No, my friend. I have a very special purpose in mind for this creature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must get back to work, sir.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Have you come to apply for the job?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, if it pays well.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I'll have to ask you some questions first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: How would you describe yourself in one word?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Mmmm, I see.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I don't think you have the aptitude for this position.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Mmmm, I see.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Just one more question. What would you say is your greatest skill?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Combat.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Mmmm, I see.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I don't think you have the aptitude for this position.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Mmmm, I see.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Looks like you're just the [man/woman] for the job! Welcome aboard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything you'd like me to do for you, sir?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Yes, there is. You're highly skilled at grave-digging, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...yes, that's what I said.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Excellent. Now listen carefully.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I need you to find some...stuff...for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stuff?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: That's what I said...stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of stuff?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well...dead stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on...

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I need you to get me enough dead body parts for me to stitch together a complete body, which I plan to bring to life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...okay...if you insist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I wouldn't work here if you paid me.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Charming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about Fenkenstrain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about Fenkenstrain?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Oi could tell you a few things about old Fenky, yeah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Once, this castle were full o' good folk - my friends. Fenky was just the castle doctor you know, to the Lord and the castle folk.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: I don't know what happened to them all, but one by one they all disappeared. When they were gone a while, I went an dug graves for 'em, in the forest.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: After a while there weren't no-one left, but the Lord, Fenkenstrain, and meself.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Old Fenky sent me into the Forest to dig 'im a pit. Never said what for. Then would you believe it, someone chops me head off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see who did it, before ...

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Before oi kicked the bucket, you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Don't worry yourself, I'm not worried about bein' dead. Worse things could happen, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: One thing I do know is, there ain't no Lord of the castle anymore, 'cept for old Fenky. Makes you think a bit, don't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about Fenkenstrain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to your head?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Oi was in the old 'aunted Forest to the south, diggin' a pit for moi old maaster, old Fenkenstrain, when would you believe it, someone chops me head off. Awful bad luck, weren't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, dreadful bad luck.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: So oi thinks to meself, I don't needs any 'ead to be gettin on with me gardenin', long as I got me hands and me spade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you show me where the place was?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: No mate, you've found my head already, oi think.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Well, oi s'pose oi've got 10 minutes to spare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about Fenkenstrain?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Me name? It's been a moivellous long while, mate, since I had any use for such a thing as a name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I was just trying to make conversation.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: No, no, I can't be havin' that - I'll remember in a minute-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, don't worry.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Lestwit, that's it! Ed Lestwit!

sections.title: Talking to the Gardener Ghost while he is following the player

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: You need to head off to them Haunted Woods, mate.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Go [direction], mate.

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: This is the place where I met me' maker.

sections.title: Entering an area other than Fenkenstrain's Castle or the Haunted woods

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Oi'm afraid you've gone well off the beaten track..

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Fare thee well - oi must return to me' garden.

sections.title: Grave north of the Fairy Ring
Grave west of the Fairy Ring
Grave south of the Fairy Ring
Grave east of the Fairy Ring

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: This is the place where I met me' maker.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: You're interested in our speciality, I see. Would you like to buy some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly is in the jar?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Pickled brain, my friend. Only 50 gold to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...pickled brain from what animal?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Animal? Don't be disgusting, man! No, this is a human brain - only the best for my customers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds very nice, but I'm afraid I don't have enough gold at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll buy one, please.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: A very wise choice, [sir/miss]. Don't eat it all at once, savour every morsel - that's my advice to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I'm not really hungry at the moment.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: You can leave that alone, my friend. I've already sold you one of your own - eat that. I can't afford to give away freebies in this business!

sections.title: Finding the torso, arms, and legs
Using the zombie head on Dr Fenkenstrain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a winner for you here, Doctor! Look! It's still moving!

lines.speaker: Zombie Head
lines.text: Nobody knows the troubles I've seen...nobody knows my sorrow...

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I can't use that head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: If that thing keeps wriggling around, I'll never get the stitches straight.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: ...And who knows what problems will come up if we have an undead head on a truly dead body.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Bring me another head, one that isn't still moving

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain with the body parts

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Where are my needle and thread, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Ah, a needle. Wonderful.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Some thread, excellent.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Where's my thread, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Where's my needle, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Perfect. But I need one more thing from you - flesh and bones by themselves do not make life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I have honed to perfection an ancient ritual that will give life to this creature, but for this I must harness the very power of Nature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what power is this?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: The power of lightning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, can't make lightning, you've got the wrong man-

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Silence your insolent tongue! The storm that brews overhead will create the lightning. What I need you to do is to repair the lightning conductor on the balcony above.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Repair the lightning conductor, right. Can I have a break, soon? By law I'm entitled to 15 minutes every-

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Repair the conductor and BEGONE!!!

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain again after sewing the body parts together

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair the lightning conductor?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Oh, it would be easier to do it myself! If you find a conductor mould you should be able to cast a new one.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Remember this, [player name]: my experiment will only work with a conductor made from silver.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some body parts for you.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Fantastic, you've brought me a head.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Splendid, you've brought me a torso.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Great, you've brought me some arms.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Excellent, you've brought me some legs.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Superb!! Those are all the parts I need. Now to sew them together ...

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Go and get me a needle, and I'll need 5 lots of thread.

sections.title: Getting help from the Gardener Ghost

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where the key to the shed is?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Got it right 'ere in my pocket. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can find a lightning conductor mould?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: A conductive mould, you say? Let me see-

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: There used to be a bloke 'ere, sort of an 'andyman e was. Did everything round the place - fixed what was broke, swept the chimneys and the like. He would 'ave had a mould, I imagine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is he now?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: E's dead, just like everyone else round 'ere ... 'cept for me.

sections.title: Using something suitable to combine the canes/brush
With the rope in the inventory
With the bowstring/sinew in the inventory
With the ball of wool in the inventory
With the bronze wire in the inventory
Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain with the conductor

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Very good, you have made a new conductor - now get up on the roof and repair it before this storm passes!

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain after using the conductor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So did it work, then?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Yes, I'm afraid it did, LogWarden - all too well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't see it anywhere.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I tricked it into going up to the Tower, and there it remains, imprisoned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the creature wasn't all you'd hoped, then?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: ...oh, what have I done...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see, we're developing a sense of right and wrong now are we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bit late for that, I'd say.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I have no control over it! It's coming to get me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do about it?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Destroy it!!! Take the key to the Tower and take back the life I never should have granted!!!

sections.title: Talking to the Gardener Ghost after using the conductor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Gardener Ghost
lines.text: Same as ever, mate, just getting' on with it regardless.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good for you...err...mate.

sections.title: Talking to the Creature of Fenkenstrain
Talking to the Creature of Fenkenstrain again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how I can stop Fenkenstrain's experiments?

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: Take the Ring of Charos from him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this ring?

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: It was my birthright, passed down to me through the ages, its origin forgotten.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: The Ring of Charos has many powers, but Fenkenstrain has bent them to his own evil purposes. Without the power of the ring, he will not be able to raise the dead from their sleep.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: It has one other, extremely important use - it confuses the werewolves' senses, making them believe that they smell one of their own kind. Without the ring, Fenkenstrain will be at their mercy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am commanded to destroy you, creature!

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: Oh that's *hic* not very *hic* nice ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you feeling okay?

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: Abso *hic* lutely. Never *buuurrp* better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look very dangerous.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: How *hic* do I look?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really don't know, do you? Have a look for yourself.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: AAAAARRGGGGHHHH!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry. I suppose I'm partly to blame for this.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: No - it was him I wager - Fenkenstrain - wasn't it? He's brought me back to life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are - were - you?

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: I was Rologarth, Lord of the North Coast - this castle was once mine. Fenkenstrain was the castle doctor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the castle wasn't really abandoned when he found it?

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: Is that what he told you? No, no, this castle was once full of people and life. Fenkenstrain advised me to sell them to the vampyres, which - I am sad to say - I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found your brain in a jar in Canifis, so he must have sold you too.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: Of that I will not speak. There lie memories that should rest with the dead, the living unable to bear them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it - I'm leaving this dreadful place, whether I get paid or not. Is there anything I can do for you before I leave?

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain's Monster
lines.text: Only one - please stop Fenkenstrain from carrying on his experiments, once and for all, so that no other poor soul has to endure suffering such as that of my people and I.

sections.title: Convincing Dr Fenkenstrain to stop his experiments

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: So have you destroyed it?!!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never, now that he has told me the truth!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Oh my, oh my, this is exactly what I feared!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Why did you have to pick Rologarth's brain of all brains?!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm through working for you.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: No!! I refuse to release you!! You must help me build another creature to destroy this dreadful mistake!!

lines.speaker: Rologarth
lines.text: How goes it, friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I stole the Ring of Charos from Fenkenstrain.

lines.speaker: Rologarth
lines.text: I saw him climb up into the Tower to hide it. It doesn't matter - soon the werewolves will come for him, and his experiments will be forever ceased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want the ring back? It is yours after all.

lines.speaker: Rologarth
lines.text: No, you keep it, my friend. Werewolves hunger for the scent of live flesh - I have no need for the ring. I have my castle back, if not my soul.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: theyrecomingtogetme theyrecomingtogetme...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is all you deserve. Lord Rologarth is master of this castle once more. Let him protect you - if he wants to.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: theyrecomingtogetme theyrecomingtogetme...
